name: whisrs
title: Whisrs
description: Control whisrs, the Linux-first voice-to-text dictation tool. Check status, view transcription history, toggle recording, read selection aloud, and manage the daemon.
author: thomasboom

commands.name: status
commands.title: Whisrs Status
commands.subtitle: Show daemon & recording state
commands.description: Query the whisrs daemon and show whether it is running, recording, and the active backend.

commands.name: history
commands.title: Transcription History
commands.subtitle: Browse recent transcriptions
commands.description: Show recent whisrs transcription history with copy and paste actions.

commands.name: toggle
commands.title: Toggle Recording
commands.subtitle: Start/stop dictation
commands.description: Press your hotkey to start recording; press again to stop and transcribe. Falls back to running the whisrs CLI.

commands.name: speak
commands.title: Read Selection Aloud
commands.subtitle: Text-to-speech via whisrs
commands.description: Read the selected text aloud using whisrs TTS. Press again to stop.

commands.name: setup
commands.title: Whisrs Setup
commands.subtitle: Interactive onboarding
commands.description: Run the interactive whisrs setup (backend, API keys, mic test, keybindings).

commands.name: restart
commands.title: Restart Daemon
commands.subtitle: Restart whisrsd
commands.description: Restart the whisrs daemon, using the systemd user service when present.

preferences.name: binaryPath
preferences.title: Whisrs Binary Path
preferences.description: Absolute path to the whisrs executable. Leave empty to use the one found in your PATH.

preferences.name: historyLimit
preferences.title: History Limit
preferences.description: How many transcription entries to fetch in the history command.
